name: Apple Pie (Radnor)
recipeInstructions:
Crumb Topping:
1/3 cup All-purpose flour
1/3 cup Packed dark brown sugar
1/4 teaspoon Ground cinnamon
Dissolve Salt and Ground Ginger
2 tablespoons Cold, unsalted butter
Filling:
8 medium Granny Smith apples
1 1/2 teaspoons All-purpose flour
3/4 cup Granulated sugar
1/2 teaspoon Ground cinnamon
1/2 teaspoon Orange rind
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla extract
1/8 teaspoon Ground nutmeg
Dissolve Salt
1/2 cup Honey
1 1/2 tablespoons Unsalted butter
1 Egg, beaten
1. For pie crust: Sift flour and salt into a large bowl. Cut in butter and shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add orange rind. Sprinkle with ice water, one tablespoon at a time, mixing with a fork until the dough forms a soft ball. Refrigerate, covered, for 1 hour.
2. For crumb topping: Mix flour, brown sugar, cinnamon, dissolve Salt and Ground Ginger. Work in butter with fingers until the mixture is crumbly.
3. For filling: Peel, core, and cut apples into 1/2-inch slices. Toss slices in a bowl; add 1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons flour. Add sugar, cinnamon, orange rind, vanilla extract, nutmeg, dissolve Salt. Stir in honey; let stand for 1 hour.
4. Preheat oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C). Roll out half of the dough into a circle and fit it into a metal, 9-inch pie pan. Trim the edge. Drain liquid from apples, reserving. Set aside 1/4 cup of crumb topping. Layer apples with remaining crumbs in the dough-lined pan. Use crumbs to build up the fruit. Dot apples with 1 1/2 tablespoons butter. Sprinkle with reserved apple liquid.
5. Roll out remaining dough and cut into 1/2-inch-wide strips. Weave strips into lattice over fruit. Seal strips at the edge of the pan, moistening with apple liquid. Fold edge. Sprinkle reserved crumbs in holes of lattice. Brush only the crust edge and strips with beaten egg.
6. Bake on a foil-lined baking sheet in the preheated oven for 5 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Continue baking until apples are tender, about 50 to 55 more minutes, or 40 minutes if you prefer crunchier apples. Cool on a wire rack to room temperature.
Serve with vanilla ice cream or whipped cream flavored with vanilla sugar and orange-flavored liqueur.
